metadata.convention: Higher S/G (more syringyl, less guaiacyl) = increased; lower = decreased. WT Populus trichocarpa stem xylem S/G ~2-3.

perturbations.notes: Canonical positive-control S/G OE experiment in poplar (Meyermans 2000; Huntley 2003; Stewart 2009; Skyba 2013)

evidence.title: The Effects on Lignin Structure of Overexpression of Ferulate 5-Hydroxylase in Hybrid Poplar
evidence.authors: Stewart JJ, Akiyama T, Chapple C, Ralph J, Mansfield SD
evidence.evidence_sentence: C4H::F5H transgenic lignin was comprised of as much as 97.5% syringyl units; the syringyl level in the wild-type control was 68%
evidence.claim: C4H::F5H OE gives dramatic S/G increase

perturbations.reconciliation_note: F5H1 OE (C4H::F5H or 35S::F5H) -> network node F5H1 gm=2.0

perturbations.notes: Canonical positive-control S/G OE experiment in poplar (Meyermans 2000; Huntley 2003; Stewart 2009; Skyba 2013)

evidence.title: Syringyl-Rich Lignin Renders Poplars More Resistant to Degradation by Wood Decay Fungi
evidence.authors: Skyba O, Douglas CJ, Mansfield SD
evidence.evidence_sentence: Line 64 exhibits ca. 94 mol%, line 82 exhibits ca. 85 mol%, and the wild type exhibits ca. 65 mol% of syringyl lignin units, as determined by thioacidolysis
evidence.claim: C4H::F5H OE dramatically increases S/G

perturbations.reconciliation_note: F5H1 OE (C4H::F5H or 35S::F5H) -> network node F5H1 gm=2.0

perturbations.notes: Canonical positive-control S/G OE experiment in poplar (Meyermans 2000; Huntley 2003; Stewart 2009; Skyba 2013)

evidence.title: Modified lignin in tobacco and poplar plants over-expressing the Arabidopsis gene encoding ferulate 5-hydroxylase
evidence.authors: Meyermans H, Morreel K, Lapierre C, Pollet B, De Bruyn A, Busson R, Herdewijn P, Devreese B, Van Beeumen J, Marita JM, Ralph J, Chen C, Burggraeve B, Van Montagu M, Messens E, Boerjan W
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ectopic expression of AtF5H cDNA in transgenic tobacco and poplar induces an increase in S-units in lignins
evidence.claim: F5H OE increases S-units

perturbations.reconciliation_note: F5H1 OE (C4H::F5H or 35S::F5H) -> network node F5H1 gm=2.0

perturbations.notes: Canonical positive-control S/G OE experiment in poplar (Meyermans 2000; Huntley 2003; Stewart 2009; Skyba 2013)

evidence.title: Significant increases in pulping efficiency in C4H-F5H-transformed poplars
evidence.authors: Huntley SK, Ellis D, Gilbert M, Chapple C, Mansfield SD
evidence.journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry
evidence.evidence_sentence: Analysis of poplar transformed with a C4H-F5H construct demonstrated significant increases in chemical (kraft) pulping efficiency; decreases of 23 kappa units and increases of more than 20 ISO brightness units in trees exhibiting high syringyl monomer concentrations
evidence.claim: C4H-F5H OE increases S content

perturbations.reconciliation_note: F5H1 OE (C4H::F5H or 35S::F5H) -> network node F5H1 gm=2.0

perturbations.notes: COMT1 is the syringyl-specific methylation step; silencing -> loss of S units

evidence.title: Lignification in Transgenic Poplars with Extremely Reduced Caffeic Acid O-Methyltransferase Activity
evidence.authors: Jouanin L, Goujon T, de Nadai V, Martin MT, Mila I, Vallet C, Pollet B, Yoshinaga A, Chabbert B, Petit-Conil M, Lapierre C
evidence.evidence_sentence: 70SOMT-3 line: 10-fold reduction in beta-O-4-linked S units (60% to 6%); almost complete lack of syringyl units
evidence.claim: COMT1 silencing dramatically reduces S

perturbations.reconciliation_note: COMT1 RNAi/antisense -> network node COMT1 gm=0.1

perturbations.notes: COMT1 is the syringyl-specific methylation step; silencing -> loss of S units

evidence.title: Lignification in Transgenic Poplars with Extremely Reduced Caffeic Acid O-Methyltransferase Activity
evidence.authors: Jouanin L, Goujon T, de Nadai V, Martin MT, Mila I, Vallet C, Pollet B, Yoshinaga A, Chabbert B, Petit-Conil M, Lapierre C
evidence.evidence_sentence: ASOMT antisense lines: 65-67% G vs 29-31% S (vs 33-34% G; 64-67% S in control)
evidence.claim: COMT1 antisense lowers S

perturbations.reconciliation_note: COMT1 RNAi/antisense -> network node COMT1 gm=0.1

perturbations.notes: CCoAOMT1 KD reduces total lignin but leaves S/G ratio largely unchanged

evidence.title: Essential Role of Caffeoyl Coenzyme A O-Methyltransferase in Lignin Biosynthesis in Woody Poplar Plants
evidence.authors: Zhong R, Morrison WH 3rd, Himmelsbach DS, Poole FL 2nd, Ye ZH
evidence.evidence_sentence: The reduction in lignin content was the result of a decrease in both guaiacyl and syringyl lignins (both reduced proportionally)
evidence.claim: CCoAOMT1 KD reduces total lignin without shifting S/G

perturbations.notes: CCR RNAi preferentially lowers S units in poplar (Leple 2007)

evidence.title: Downregulation of Cinnamoyl-Coenzyme A Reductase in Poplar
evidence.authors: Leple JC, Dauwe R, Morreel K, Storme V, Lapierre C, Pollet B, Naumann A, Kang KY, Kim H, Ruel K, Lefebvre A, Joseleau JP, Grima-Pettenati J, De Rycke R, Andersson-Gunneras S, Erban A, Fehrle I, Petit-Conil M, Kopka J, Polle A, Messens E, Sundberg B, Mansfield SD, Ralph J, Pilate G, Boerjan W
evidence.evidence_sentence: CCR transcript levels down to 3 to 4% of wild-type; S/G ratio of FAS13 and FS40 lignin was significantly lower than that of wild type; relatively more reduced in syringyl than in guaiacyl units
evidence.claim: CCR2 KD preferentially reduces S

perturbations.notes: Null CCR2 allele + haploinsufficient allele (De Meester 2020)

evidence.title: Tailoring poplar lignin without yield penalty by combining a null and haploinsufficient CINNAMOYL-CoA REDUCTASE2 allele
evidence.authors: De Meester B, Madariaga Calderon B, de Vries L, Pollier J, Goeminne G, Van Doorsselaere J, Chen M, Ralph J, Vanholme R, Boerjan W
evidence.evidence_sentence: Null CCR2 allele combined with haploinsufficient allele alters lignin composition
evidence.claim: CCR2 compound allele decreases S

perturbations.notes: CAD acts on both coniferaldehyde and sinapaldehyde; KD does not shift S/G ratio

evidence.title: Red Xylem and Higher Lignin Extractability by Down-Regulating a Cinnamyl Alcohol Dehydrogenase in Poplar
evidence.authors: Baucher M, Chabbert B, Pilate G, Van Doorsselaere J, Tollier MT, Petit-Conil M, Cornu D, Monties B, Van Montagu M, Inze D, Jouanin L, Boerjan W
evidence.evidence_sentence: Neither the lignin amount nor the lignin monomeric composition (syringyl/guaiacyl) were significantly modified
evidence.claim: CAD1 KD does not affect S/G

perturbations.notes: C3H KD redirects flux to H; G reduced preferentially, S maintained (Coleman 2008; Ralph 2012). Opposing result in P. alba x glandulosa (Peng 2021) reflects genetic-background variation.

evidence.title: RNAi-mediated suppression of p-coumaroyl-CoA 3'-hydroxylase in hybrid poplar impacts lignin deposition and soluble secondary metabolism
evidence.authors: Coleman HD, Park JY, Nair R, Chapple C, Mansfield SD
evidence.evidence_sentence: up to approximately 20% p-hydroxyphenyl (H) lignin subunits; increase in H monomers came at the expense of only guaiacyl (G); S maintained
evidence.claim: C3H RNAi lowers G not S, increasing S/G

perturbations.notes: C3H KD redirects flux to H; G reduced preferentially, S maintained (Coleman 2008; Ralph 2012). Opposing result in P. alba x glandulosa (Peng 2021) reflects genetic-background variation.

evidence.title: Effects on Lignin Structure of Coumarate 3-Hydroxylase Downregulation in Poplar
evidence.authors: Ralph J, Akiyama T, Coleman HD, Mansfield SD
evidence.evidence_sentence: C3H downregulation reduced lignin levels by half; G/S went from 0.59 WT to 0.28 C3H (so S/G from ~1.7 to ~3.6)
evidence.claim: C3H KD increases S/G

perturbations.notes: C3H KD redirects flux to H; G reduced preferentially, S maintained (Coleman 2008; Ralph 2012). Opposing result in P. alba x glandulosa (Peng 2021) reflects genetic-background variation.

evidence.title: Effects of P-Coumarate 3-Hydroxylase Downregulation on the Compositional and Structural Characteristics of Lignin and Hemicelluloses in Poplar Wood
evidence.authors: Peng X, Bian X, Yao S, Ma C, Wen J
evidence.evidence_sentence: the S/G ratio in DEL-CK was 2.82, while the S/G ratio for DEL-C3H was 2.48
evidence.claim: C3H KD modest S/G decrease in alt hybrid

perturbations.notes: 4CL antisense lowers total lignin and S/G

evidence.title: Antisense Down-Regulation of 4CL Expression Alters Lignification, Tree Growth, and Saccharification Potential of Field-Grown Poplar
evidence.authors: Voelker SL, Lachenbruch B, Meinzer FC, Jourdes M, Ki C, Patten AM, Davin LB, Lewis NG, Tuskan GA, Gunter L, Decker SR, Selig MJ, Sykes R, Himmel ME, Kitin P, Shevchenko O, Strauss SH
evidence.evidence_sentence: S/G ratios declined to as little as 1.1 to 1.4 in event 712 as compared with 1.8 to 2.3
evidence.claim: 4CL antisense lowers S/G

perturbations.notes: 4CL1 CRISPR KO preferentially reduces S relative to G (Wang 2020)

evidence.title: Compensatory Guaiacyl Lignin Biosynthesis at the Expense of Syringyl Lignin in 4CL1-Knockout Poplar
evidence.authors: Wang JP, Matthews ML, Williams CM, Shi R, Yang C, Tunlaya-Anukit S, Chen HC, Li Q, Liu J, Lin CY, Naik P, Sun YH, Loziuk PL, Yeh TF, Kim H, Gjersing E, Shollenberger T, Shuford CM, Song J, Miller Z, Huang YY, Edmunds CW, Liu B, Sun Y, Lin YJ, Li W, Chen H, Peszlen I, Ducoste JJ, Ralph J, Chang HM, Muddiman DC, Davis MF, Smith C, Isik F, Sederoff RR, Chiang VL
evidence.evidence_sentence: pyMBMS analysis detected a 30% decrease in S/G ratio; 34% reduction of S-units and a small (5%) reduction of G-units
evidence.claim: 4CL1 CRISPR KO lowers S/G via preferential S reduction

perturbations.notes: 4CL5 CRISPR KO does not change S/G (4CL5 is not the lignin-dedicated paralog)

evidence.title: Compensatory Guaiacyl Lignin Biosynthesis at the Expense of Syringyl Lignin in 4CL1-Knockout Poplar
evidence.authors: Wang JP, Matthews ML, Williams CM, Shi R, Yang C, Tunlaya-Anukit S, Chen HC, Li Q, Liu J, Lin CY, Naik P, Sun YH, Loziuk PL, Yeh TF, Kim H, Gjersing E, Shollenberger T, Shuford CM, Song J, Miller Z, Huang YY, Edmunds CW, Liu B, Sun Y, Lin YJ, Li W, Chen H, Peszlen I, Ducoste JJ, Ralph J, Chang HM, Muddiman DC, Davis MF, Smith C, Isik F, Sederoff RR, Chiang VL
evidence.evidence_sentence: CRISPR-KO of 4CL5 did not change either lignin content or S/G ratio
evidence.claim: 4CL5 CRISPR KO has no S/G change

perturbations.reconciliation_note: PT4CL5 removed from network per Wang 2020 (DOI 10.1104/pp.19.01550): 4CL5 CRISPR KO produces no change in lignin content or S/G ratio, so 4CL5 is not the lignin-dedicated paralog.

perturbations.notes: CSE1 RNAi modestly reduces S/G (Saleme 2017)

evidence.title: Silencing CAFFEOYL SHIKIMATE ESTERASE Affects Lignification and Improves Saccharification in Poplar
evidence.authors: Saleme MLS, Cesarino I, Vargas L, Kim H, Vanholme R, Goeminne G, Van Acker R, Fonseca FCA, Pallidis A, Voorend W, Nicomedes J Jr, Ralph J, Boerjan W
evidence.evidence_sentence: S/G ratio decreased from 2.06 in the wild type to 1.85 and 1.9 in hpCSE#1 and hpCSE#2
evidence.claim: CSE RNAi modestly lowers S/G

perturbations.notes: Both paralogs CRISPR-edited (de Vries 2021); effective full KO of CSE function

evidence.title: CRISPR-Cas9 editing of CAFFEOYL SHIKIMATE ESTERASE 1 and 2 shows their importance and partial redundancy in lignification in Populus tremula x P. alba
evidence.authors: de Vries L, Guevara-Rozo S, Cobo-Medina M, Hoengenaert L, Ando D, Keene C, Gjersing E, Vega-Sanchez ME, Ralph J, Loque D
evidence.evidence_sentence: CRISPR double KO of CSE1 and CSE2 shows importance and partial redundancy in lignification
evidence.claim: CSE1;CSE2 double KO strongly reduces lignification

perturbations.reconciliation_note: CSE2 not separately represented; CSE1 node represents the CSE family

perturbations.notes: SND1 OE activates secondary wall program; modest S/G increase via MYB/F5H1 route

evidence.title: The poplar PtrWNDs are transcriptional activators of secondary cell wall biosynthesis
evidence.authors: Zhong R, Ye ZH
evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of PtrWND2 and PtrWND6 in Arabidopsis leads to activation of secondary wall biosynthetic genes and ectopic deposition of secondary walls
evidence.claim: SND1/WND2 OE increases secondary wall biosynthesis

perturbations.notes: Dominant repression creates a chimeric repressor on SND1-family targets; treated as composite KO

evidence.title: Dissection of the Transcriptional Program Regulating Secondary Wall Biosynthesis during Wood Formation in Poplar
evidence.authors: McCarthy RL, Zhong R, Lee C, Ye ZH
evidence.evidence_sentence: Dominant repression of PtrWND2B reduced wall thickness of xylary fibers by 56%
evidence.claim: SND1 dominant-repression strongly reduces lignification

perturbations.reconciliation_note: PtrWND2B SRDX dominant-repression acts as family-level LoF -> SND1 gm=0.0

perturbations.notes: Master-switch MYB dominant-repression: 60-70% wall-thickness reduction

evidence.title: The Poplar MYB Master Switches Bind to the SMRE Site and Activate the Secondary Wall Biosynthetic Program during Wood Formation
evidence.authors: Zhong R, McCarthy RL, Haghighat M, Ye ZH
evidence.evidence_sentence: thickness of secondary walls in wood fibers in PtrMYB3 and PtrMYB21 repressors was reduced by 60 to 70%
evidence.claim: MYB3 dominant-repression strongly lowers secondary wall

perturbations.notes: Master-switch MYB dominant-repression: strong secondary-wall alteration

evidence.title: The Poplar MYB Master Switches Bind to the SMRE Site and Activate the Secondary Wall Biosynthetic Program during Wood Formation
evidence.authors: Zhong R, McCarthy RL, Haghighat M, Ye ZH
evidence.evidence_sentence: strong alterations in wall thickness and vessel morphology in wood of stems
evidence.claim: MYB21 dominant-repression strongly alters secondary wall

perturbations.notes: MYB152 OE (Populus TF expressed in Arabidopsis or Populus)

evidence.title: Regulation of secondary cell wall biosynthesis by poplar R2R3 MYB transcription factor PtrMYB152 in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Wang S, Li E, Porth I, Chen JG, Mansfield SD, Douglas CJ
evidence.evidence_sentence: 24% and 18% increase in lignin for 35S:PtrMYB152 and PtrCesA8:PtrMYB152; S/G monomer ratio higher (0.37-0.42) vs wild-type (0.31)
evidence.claim: MYB152 OE raises S/G

perturbations.notes: MYB156 OE (Populus TF expressed in Arabidopsis or Populus)

evidence.title: PtoMYB156 is involved in negative regulation of phenylpropanoid metabolism and secondary cell wall biosynthesis during wood formation in poplar
evidence.authors: Yang L, Zhao X, Ran L, Li C, Fan D, Luo K
evidence.evidence_sentence: Transgenic plants overexpressing PtoMYB156 displayed a dramatic decrease in secondary wall thicknesses of xylem fibers and the content of cellulose, lignin and xylose compared with wild-type
evidence.claim: MYB156 OE strongly represses secondary wall and lignin

perturbations.notes: MYB221 OE (Populus TF expressed in Arabidopsis or Populus)

evidence.title: Poplar PdMYB221 is involved in the direct and indirect regulation of secondary wall biosynthesis during wood formation
evidence.authors: Tang X, Zhuang Y, Qi G, Wang D, Liu H, Wang K, Chai G, Zhou G
evidence.evidence_sentence: 35S:PdMYB221: lignin decreased 28%, cellulose 10%, xylose 26%; plants cannot remain upright
evidence.claim: MYB221 OE represses lignin

perturbations.notes: KNAT7 OE: slight S/G increase (Ahlawat 2021)

evidence.title: Genetic Modification of KNAT7 Transcription Factor Expression Enhances Saccharification and Reduces Recalcitrance of Woody Biomass in Poplars
evidence.authors: Ahlawat YK, Biswal AK, Chowdary YA, Mohnen D
evidence.evidence_sentence: No significant changes in lignin content; 5-7% increase in S/G ratios
evidence.claim: KNAT7 OE gives slight S/G increase

perturbations.notes: KNAT7 antisense also gives slight S/G increase (KNAT7 is a modulatory brake; both OE and KD shift composition slightly)

evidence.title: Genetic Modification of KNAT7 Transcription Factor Expression Enhances Saccharification and Reduces Recalcitrance of Woody Biomass in Poplars
evidence.authors: Ahlawat YK, Biswal AK, Chowdary YA, Mohnen D
evidence.evidence_sentence: significant decrease of about 6% lignin; 8-12% increase in S/G ratios
evidence.claim: KNAT7 antisense gives slight S/G increase with lignin drop

perturbations.notes: LTF1 is a poplar MYB repressor regulated by phosphorylation; no direct ortholog node in the network

evidence.title: Phosphorylation of LTF1, an MYB Transcription Factor in Populus, Acts as a Sensory Switch Regulating Lignin Biosynthesis in Wood Cells
evidence.authors: Gui J, Luo L, Zhong Y, Sun J, Umezawa T, Li L
evidence.evidence_sentence: Expression of a phosphorylation-null mutant version of LTF1 led to stable protein accumulation and persistent attenuation of lignification in wood cells
evidence.claim: LTF1 phospho-null mutant reduces lignification

perturbations.reconciliation_note: LTF1 (MYB lignin repressor; Gui 2019) is not represented in the current network

perturbations.notes: NF-YA11 activates F5H; OE raises S/G (Wei 2024)

evidence.title: Poplar NF-YA11 alters lignin composition and increases methane yield by upregulating the FERULIC ACID 5-HYDROXYLASE 2 gene
evidence.authors: Wei et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: S/G lignin ratio was increased in PtNF-YA11-overexpressing poplars
evidence.claim: NF-YA11 OE increases S/G

perturbations.notes: nf-ya11 poplars show reduced S-lignin and S/G ratio (Wei 2024)

evidence.title: Poplar NF-YA11 alters lignin composition and increases methane yield by upregulating the FERULIC ACID 5-HYDROXYLASE 2 gene
evidence.authors: Wei et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: CRISPR-Cas9 edited nf-ya11 poplars exhibit a decrease in S-lignin composition and S/G ratio
evidence.claim: NF-YA11 CRISPR KO decreases S/G

perturbations.notes: Ptr-miR397a OE lowers total lignin via laccase suppression; S/G unchanged (Lu 2013)

evidence.title: Ptr-miR397a is a negative regulator of laccase genes affecting lignin content in Populus trichocarpa
evidence.authors: Lu S, Li Q, Wei H, Chang MJ, Tunlaya-Anukit S, Kim H, Liu J, Song J, Sun YH, Yuan L, Yeh TF, Peszlen I, Ralph J, Sederoff RR, Chiang VL
evidence.evidence_sentence: S/G ratio of 2.2 compared with 2.1 in WT. The difference was not significant
evidence.claim: miR397a OE reduces lignin but leaves S/G unchanged

perturbations.reconciliation_note: miR397a targets laccase genes; neither miR397a nor laccase nodes are in the network

perturbations.notes: SND1-B (WND6B) DR gave 68% wall reduction; collapsed to SND1 composite

evidence.title: Dissection of the Transcriptional Program Regulating Secondary Wall Biosynthesis during Wood Formation in Poplar
evidence.authors: McCarthy RL, Zhong R, Lee C, Ye ZH
evidence.evidence_sentence: Dominant repression of PtrWND6B reduced wall thickness by 68%
evidence.claim: SND1-B (WND6B) DR reduces secondary wall

perturbations.reconciliation_note: PtrWND6B is an SND1 paralog; SND1 node represents the family. Dominant-repression acts on family targets -> gm=0.0.

perturbations.notes: HCT RNAi lowers total lignin without shifting S/G (Peng 2014; Zhou 2018)

evidence.title: Preliminary study of Cell Wall Structure and its Mechanical Properties of C3H and HCT RNAi Transgenic Poplar Sapling
evidence.authors: Zhou X, Ren S, Lu M, Zhao S, Chen Z, Zhao R, Lv J
evidence.evidence_sentence: lignin content of HCT samples was 9.4% lower than non-modified samples; no significant differences in S/G ratio and the distribution of the inter-unit linkages between the control and HCT down-regulated poplar
evidence.claim: HCT RNAi lowers total lignin but does not shift S/G

evidence.title: Structural characterization of lignins from hydroxycinnamoyl transferase (HCT) down-regulated transgenic poplars
evidence.authors: Peng XP, Sun SL, Wen JL, Yin WL, Sun RC
evidence.evidence_sentence: There were no significant differences in S/G ratio between the control and HCT down-regulated poplar
evidence.claim: HCT RNAi does not change S/G

perturbations.notes: Tension-wood lignin is S-enriched relative to normal/opposite wood (Al-Haddad 2013)

evidence.title: Chemical responses to modified lignin composition in tension wood of hybrid poplar (Populus tremula x Populus alba)
evidence.authors: Al-Haddad JM, Kang KY, Mansfield SD, Telewski FW
evidence.evidence_sentence: In the wild-type poplar, the lignin in tension wood contained a significantly higher percentage of syringyl than normal wood or opposite wood
evidence.claim: Tension wood formation in WT poplar elevates S/G relative to normal or opposite wood

perturbations.reconciliation_note: Tension wood induction encoded as Tension_Wood source node with exogenous_supply=1.0

perturbations.notes: Negative control: no modifications, expected=unchanged

evidence.title: Lignification in Transgenic Poplars with Extremely Reduced Caffeic Acid O-Methyltransferase Activity (WT control)
evidence.authors: Jouanin L et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: Wild-type Populus tremula x alba stem xylem: ~33-34% G, 64-67% S; S/G ratio ~2.0
evidence.claim: WT baseline S/G ratio in P. tremula x alba stem xylem

perturbations.reconciliation_note: WT (no perturbation) - P. tremula x alba baseline

perturbations.notes: Negative control for the reference species

evidence.title: Ptr-miR397a is a negative regulator of laccase genes affecting lignin content in Populus trichocarpa (WT control)
evidence.authors: Lu S et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: S/G ratio of 2.1 in WT Populus trichocarpa stem xylem
evidence.claim: WT baseline S/G in P. trichocarpa ~2.0-3.0

perturbations.reconciliation_note: WT (no perturbation) - P. trichocarpa reference species baseline

perturbations.notes: Negative control for heterologous Arabidopsis assays (MYB152/MYB221 OE)

evidence.title: Regulation of secondary cell wall biosynthesis by poplar R2R3 MYB transcription factor PtrMYB152 in Arabidopsis (WT control)
evidence.authors: Wang S et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: Arabidopsis wild-type controls showed S/G 0.31 vs 0.37-0.42 in 35S:PtrMYB152 lines
evidence.claim: Arabidopsis WT baseline for heterologous expression assays

perturbations.reconciliation_note: WT (no perturbation) - Arabidopsis heterologous-expression host baseline
